Universe — names screened today. Aligned-Bull / Aligned-Bear — all present lenses (screen · technicals · options sentiment · dealer gamma) agree up / down. Conflicted — a strong bull and a strong bear lens on the same name. Screen hits — names passing the core contrarian screen (forward-EPS growth + consensus PT not stretched + ≥3 analysts).

COT Positioning Regime

#8 Early Risk-Off / Defensive Rotation

Investors are reducing risk gradually — not panicking, but rotating toward safety. The sequence matters: small caps weaken first, growth weakens next, and institutions continue holding core large-cap equities as the last line of defense. This is a deterioration-in-progress regime. It often precedes broader weakness if the ES pillar eventually cracks. The market is telling you that credit-sensitive and speculative segments are already under stress, even while the S&P 500 looks relatively composed. A classic "calm before the storm" pattern.

Dashboard Output

FieldRead
Equity appetiteNeutral
LeadershipLarge Cap (by default)
Economic viewDeteriorating
BreadthNarrowing
ConvictionDivergent
CrowdingModerate (short-side building)
PositioningDefensive Rotation
Confirmation layers
LayerWhat it measuresThis weekHow it's graded
Composite scoreSingle 0–100 blend of the positioning + price/vol picture: 0.35·ES + 0.20·RTY combined positioning + 0.20·breadth percentile + 0.15·(100 − VIX percentile) + 0.10·conviction.43.2 → Neutral0–20 Panic · 20–40 Risk-Off · 40–60 Neutral · 60–80 Risk-On · 80–100 Euphoria
Layer 2 — Breadth confirmationEqual-weight ÷ cap-weight S&P 500 (^SPXEW ÷ ^GSPC): is the average stock keeping up with the mega-caps?Narrowing (mega-cap led) (pct 32.7)ratio rising > +0.5% over ~13 weeks = Broadening (healthy); falling < −0.5% = Narrowing (mega-cap led); otherwise Flat
Layer 3 — VIX positioning (COT)Combined asset-manager + leveraged-fund net-position percentile in VIX futures: are participants owning or abandoning volatility hedges?Neutral (pct 32.7)VIX combined pct > 80 = Fear (defensive) · < 20 = Complacency (bullish-but-fragile) · 20–80 = Neutral. Combined with equities: ES > 70 & VIX < 30 = Confident Risk-On; ES > 70 & VIX > 70 = Hedged Risk-On; ES < 30 & VIX > 80 = Panic
Layer 4 — Positioning vs priceES combined positioning percentile vs where SPY price actually sits.AlignedES combined < 30 while SPY within 2% of its high = Bullish divergence (under-positioned into strength); ES > 70 while SPY's 4-week return ≤ 0 = Bearish divergence (crowded, no follow-through); otherwise Aligned
Regime matrixPrice + positioning cross-tab (ES/RTY positioning × breadth direction × VIX) mapped to a single named market state.Narrow Risk-One.g. Broad Risk-On, Narrow Risk-On, Defensive Risk-On, Breadth Expansion, Hedged Bull Market, Neutral, Risk-Off, Panic, Euphoria

04Forward-Looking Signals

Predictive / lower-precision — kept deliberately SEPARATE from the confirmation verdict above so noisy anticipation signals never dilute the clean trend reads. Options-implied expected moves with a dealer-gamma/skew direction, then the technical coiled-spring precursors that flag a move before it confirms.

Expected Moves — directional options read

How to read
Spot
Current price.
Exp. range
The ≈1σ band the options market implies price travels by the near expiry.
Move
Size of that implied move (±%).
Bias
Directional lean from dealer gamma / skew — ↑ up · ↓ down · → neutral.
Adv. rec
The expected-move engine's call — STRONG_BUY / BUY · HOLD · SELL / STRONG_SELL.
Confidence
Trust in the directional read — ●●● High · ●●○ Moderate · ●○○ Low · ○○○ Very Low.
Gamma target
Dealer magnet price is pulled toward, with source (call_wall = resistance · put_wall = support · zero_gamma = gamma-flip).

Coiled-Spring Watch — technical precursors firing

How to read
Watch
Overall coil read — bull-coil (upward lean) / bear-coil (downward lean) when set; else the raw precursor tags firing — sqz squeeze · dry volume dry-up · +div / −div pending divergence · ma-tight MA compression.
Squeeze
✓ = volatility squeeze on (Bollinger/Keltner bands compressed — coiled range).
Vol-Dry
✓ = volume has dried up (participation contracting, fuel building for a break).
Pending Div
A divergence building but not yet confirmed — +div bullish · −div bearish.
RS vs SPY
Relative-strength slope vs SPY — positive outperforming · negative lagging.
Tech Target
Technical band — ↑$ upside · ↓$ downside · stop = ATR invalidation.
